message:
perf(func): apply text_arg fast-path to ConcatFunc + add ignored bench

Mirror commit to the `concat_ws` change earlier this session, this
time on the plain `concat(...)` builtin.

`ConcatFunc::invoke` was building its joined output as:

    for arg in args {
        if !arg.is_null() {
            result.push_str(&arg.to_text());
        }
    }

The `arg.to_text()` call materializes a `Cow<'_, str>` via the
public `SqliteValue::to_text()` path, which forces an allocation
for already-text values that could otherwise be borrowed. The
`text_arg(arg)` helper at line 1724 returns the same Cow shape but
takes the borrow path when the value is already a `SmallText`,
avoiding the clone-into-String trip.

Switch to:

    result.push_str(text_arg(arg).as_ref());

Same semantics — `concat` still treats NULL as empty string (unlike
`||` which propagates NULL), still builds the result in-place into
a single `String` accumulator. The win is the dropped allocation
per text arg.

Also adds `#[ignore = "perf-only benchmark"] fn perf_concat_text_args()`
mirroring the `perf_concat_ws_text_args` benchmark added earlier.
Same harness shape:

  - 24-text-arg payload, 50k invocations, 5 repeats,
    `best_ns = min(repeats)`.
  - `std::hint::black_box` around the args slice and the result.
  - Exhaustive `match` on `SqliteValue` for the result-length
    capture so a future variant addition forces an explicit
    decision here rather than silently skipping.

Lives in `mod tests` rather than `benches/` for the same reasons
the `concat_ws` bench does — `ConcatFunc` is private, the
function-registry harness duplication isn't worth it for a single
hot-path measurement, and `cargo test --release perf_concat_text_args
-- --ignored --nocapture` is the convention for this crate's perf
checks.

Pairs with the earlier `concat_ws` text-arg fast-path commit so the
two related builtins now share both the runtime path AND the bench
shape — running them together gives a side-by-side budget for the
two most common concatenation cases.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,52 @@
+# concat Direct Text Append Perf Proof
+
+Date: 2026-04-28
+Agent: IcyBluff
+Baseline source commit: bbdb93f1 perf(func): publish concat_ws direct append proof
+Candidate code: this commit
+
+## Candidate
+
+Replace per-argument `arg.to_text()` in `concat` with direct appends through
+`text_arg(arg).as_ref()`. Text arguments borrow their existing `SmallText`
+contents instead of allocating one `String` per argument. `NULL` remains skipped
+as an empty argument, matching the existing `concat` behavior.
+
+## Benchmark Command
+
+Baseline:
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-icybluff-20260428-concat-baseline cargo test -p fsqlite-func perf_concat_text_args -- --ignored --nocapture
+```
+
+Candidate:
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-icybluff-20260428-concat-candidate cargo test -p fsqlite-func perf_concat_text_args -- --ignored --nocapture
+```
+
+Raw output: `baseline.txt`, `candidate.txt`.
+
+## Results
+
+| Benchmark | Baseline best | Candidate best | Delta |
+| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
+| `perf_concat_text_args` | 50,571,320 ns | 20,362,527 ns | -59.735% |
+
+Workload: 50,000 invocations, 24 text arguments, 5 repeats. Output length stayed
+168 bytes.
+
+## Verification
+
+```bash
+cargo fmt --check
+git diff --check
+rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-icybluff-20260428-concat-verify cargo test -p fsqlite-func concat -- --nocapture
+rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-icybluff-20260428-concat-verify cargo check --workspace --all-targets
+rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-icybluff-20260428-concat-verify cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
+ubs crates/fsqlite-func/src/builtins.rs tests/artifacts/perf/20260428T2110Z-icybluff-concat-direct-text/RESULT.md
+```
+
+All verification commands passed. UBS exited 0 with 0 critical issues; it also
+reported the existing warning inventory for `builtins.rs`.
